interactions.summary: Talabani closes by urging patience, continued Pakistan-backed negotiations, and recognition that violence will not resolve the conflict. The final segment is Piers Morgan Uncensored outro and subscription copy, not source argument.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Piers Morgan and guests; diarization is UNKNOWN throughout, attribution inferred from introductions and direct address.

speaker_notes.note: Machine speaker labels are all UNKNOWN; speaker turns are inferred from host questions, named introductions, and direct address rather than diarization.

claims.claim: Talabani says Pakistan sending a team to Tehran gives reason for optimism and that one bad day should not end negotiations.
He says progress was made on smaller issues and ceasefire deadlines can be extended if people see that violence will not resolve the conflict.

signature_moments.moment: The final substantive line rejects violence as the solution to the conflict.
signature_moments.source_phrase: this is one of those conflicts that violence will not resolve
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This phrase carries the packet argument in compact public-readable form.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A negotiable timeline Talabani says is not written in stone and can be extended.

chronology_notes.note: Segment 0098 is show outro/subscription material and should not be treated as Jiang or guest argument.

uncertainty_notes.note: Outro copy is publisher boilerplate, not substantive interview content.
